description_long: This is a broken fragment of a stone, likely a piece of glazed ceramic or pottery, with a roughly triangular shape and a thick, uneven cross-section. The front surface displays a faded blue painted pattern on a white background, with visible crackling and crazing in the glaze, suggesting age and exposure. The edges are jagged and irregular, indicating it was once part of a larger object, possibly a decorative tile or vessel. The back and sides are rough, unglazed, and show a natural stone or ceramic texture with some dirt and discoloration. There are faint markings or writing on the back, possibly cataloging information. The overall appearance is weathered, with chips and surface wear consistent with long-term outdoor or archaeological exposure.
description: A weathered, broken stone or ceramic fragment with blue glaze and visible crackling, featuring jagged edges and a rough, unglazed back.
description_view_0: The front view shows the blue painted pattern on a white, crackled glazed surface, with the most detail and color visible.
description_view_1: The side view reveals the thickness of the fragment and the rough, stone-like texture of the edge.
description_view_2: The back view displays the unglazed, rough surface with some writing or markings and visible chips.
description_view_3: The opposite side view highlights the irregular, jagged edge and the transition between glazed and unglazed surfaces.
attribute_location: archaeological site or historic urban park
attribute_placement: lying loose on the ground or displayed as a fragment
attribute_pattern: faded blue painted pattern on a crackled white glaze, irregular and partial due to breakage
attribute_weathering: cracked and eroded glaze with visible surface crazing and chipped edges
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: contrasts with natural ground or modern surfaces, may be partially embedded in soil or displayed on a plinth
